text: Working with APIs is an essential skill for modern developers. An API allows different software applications to communicate with each other. Typically, you send HTTP requests like GET to retrieve data or POST to submit new information. The server often responds with data in JSON format, which is easy for programs to read. Many APIs require authentication using an API key, so you must include it in your request header. You also need to be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time period. Finally, proper error handling is crucial. Always check the response status code and handle errors like 404 or 500 gracefully to make your application robust.
